name: Loan For Any Purpose
description_short: Loan For Any Purpose Albuquerque, New Mexico — Loan For Any Purpose offers cash advances, payday loans, title loans, and personal loans from $250–$50,00...
description_long:
Loan For Any Purpose is a multi-product consumer lender operating physical branch locations in Detroit, MI (3403 Grand River Ave) and Atlanta, GA (2197 Cheshire Bridge Rd NE). The company markets itself as a personal loan provider serving borrowers across the credit spectrum, from those with damaged credit to those with higher scores seeking larger unsecured loans. Their extended branch hours — weekdays 7am to 8pm and weekends 9am to 6pm — suggest a focus on working borrowers who cannot visit lenders during standard business hours.

The company offers seven distinct loan products: cash advances for under $1,000, payday loans up to $1,000, same-day loans with minimal documentation, title loans secured by a vehicle title up to $50,000, installment loans between $1,000 and $5,000, general bad credit loans from $250 to $50,000, and unsecured personal loans up to $50,000 (the last requiring a higher credit score). This breadth allows borrowers to find a product at nearly any need or credit tier, from a quick $250 cash advance to a $50,000 secured title loan.

What distinguishes Loan For Any Purpose from strictly online lenders is the brick-and-mortar presence with staffed branches and a local phone number — useful for borrowers who prefer face-to-face service or need assistance navigating options. The company also states that submitting an application will not affect a borrower's FICO score, which is notable. However, they disclose that they may pull data from a non-traditional consumer reporting agency, meaning an inquiry may appear on that agency's report even if FICO is unaffected.

Honestly, the website discloses very little about actual rates, APRs, or fees, stating only that 'rates, terms, fees, and availability vary by state and applicant.' This opacity is a meaningful drawback. The site also prominently notes that loan products are designed for short-term needs and are not intended as long-term borrowing solutions — a standard disclaimer for lenders whose rates are typically high. Borrowers with ongoing credit difficulties are directed to seek nonprofit credit counseling, which is a responsible disclosure but also an implicit acknowledgment that these products may be expensive.
pros: Accepts all credit types for most products, including bad credit borrowers
Wide loan range from $250 to $50,000 across multiple product types
Same-day loans available with minimum documentation required
Applying does not affect your FICO credit score
In-person branch locations in Detroit and Atlanta for face-to-face service
Title loans available for borrowers who need larger amounts and have a vehicle as collateral
cons: No rates, APRs, or fee disclosures on the website — borrowers cannot comparison shop without applying
Explicitly positioned as a short-term lender, not suitable for long-term financial needs
May pull from a non-traditional consumer reporting agency, which can create an inquiry on that agency's file
Personal loans up to $50,000 require a higher credit score — 'all credit types welcome' does not apply to the largest loan tier
Only two physical locations (Detroit and Atlanta) — geographic availability is limited
best_for: Bad credit borrowers in Detroit or Atlanta who need quick access to $250–$5,000
Vehicle owners needing larger loans up to $50,000 who can offer their title as collateral
Working borrowers who need evening or weekend branch hours to apply in person
Borrowers who want to apply without a hard FICO inquiry to gauge options
diagnosis: Loan For Any Purpose suits Detroit and Atlanta borrowers across a wide credit range who need amounts from a few hundred dollars to $50,000 and prefer an in-person lender with extended hours. The main caveat is full rate opacity — APRs and fees are not published, so borrowers must apply to learn the actual cost, and the short-term product focus suggests rates are likely high relative to credit unions or banks.
services: In-person branch loan applications (Detroit and Atlanta)
Online loan applications with identity verification
Non-FICO credit review via alternative consumer reporting agencies
